commit.message:
feat(marketplace): autosave on change and URL validation for Marketplace settings (#1285) Marketplace settings previously required an explicit Save/Cancel flow and allowed toggling sources on/off. This PR removes that friction: changes persist immediately on add/delete, the enable/disable toggle is gone (all present sources are always active), and new sources are fully validated before they can be added. ## Changes **`manage-marketplace-settings.tsx`** - Removed Save/Cancel bottom bar — add/delete now write directly to `marketplaceSourcesAtom` and invalidate `['marketplace']` queries immediately - Removed `Switch` toggle and the intermediate `localSources` state + `useEffect` sync - Added inline URL validator `validateMarketplaceUrl` (static checks): - Must be non-empty - Must start with `https://` (HTTP rejected) - Must end with `/marketplace.json` - Added async schema validation via `POST /api/marketplace/validate` on "Add": - Fetches the URL server-side and validates the JSON against the marketplace schema - Shows a spinner inside the Add button and disables both Cancel and Add while validating - On failure, shows an inline error with a link to the public `marketplace.json` for reference - Error cleared on next keystroke **`app/api/marketplace/validate/route.ts`** _(new)_ - `POST /api/marketplace/validate` fetches the given URL server-side and validates the JSON has `version`, `marketplace`, and `items[].name` fields - Returns `{ valid, error?, itemCount? }` **`manage-marketplace-settings.test.tsx`** - Replaced save/toggle/revert tests with autosave and validation coverage (20 tests total) - Added tests for schema validation error and fetch error cases with reference link - Fixed CodeQL `js/incomplete-hostname-regexp` alert: escaped `.` in hostname regex patterns - Fixed SonarQube warning: replaced `global.fetch = mockFetch` module-level global mutation with `vi.stubGlobal('fetch', mockFetch)` inside `beforeEach`; removed unused `type Mock` import **`app/api/marketplace/validate/route.test.ts`** _(new)_ - 10 tests covering valid manifest, missing URL, HTTP errors, network errors, invalid JSON, missing required fields, and item without `name` ## Screenshots URL validation (static checks — HTTPS required): Validating state (spinner inside Add button) and schema error state (inline error with reference link): ## Checklist - [ ] Follow the [contributor guide](./CONTRIBUTING.md) - [ ] End-to-end tests pass - [x] Unit tests for essential parts of your code, e.g. APIs exposed via SDKs or endpoints - [ ] Update `./docs` - [ ] Recognize contributors with "@all-contributors please add for "

commit.message:
fix(auth): SBD-04/SBD-13 rate limiting + demo backdoor + error sanitization

SBD-04 — Brute-force protection:
- Added in-memory sliding-window rate limiter (5 req/60s per IP) on
  /api/auth/login and /api/auth/demo
- Returns 429 + Retry-After header on excess attempts
- No external dependency (pure stdlib: collections.deque + time.monotonic)

SBD-04 — Demo backdoor:
- /api/auth/demo now returns 404 when not in demo mode
  (PLATFORM_LLM_PROVIDER != 'demo')
- Previously any host could call this endpoint and auto-create an admin
  user with a hardcoded password — full auth bypass on prod

SBD-13 — Error info disclosure:
- setup: replaced f'Setup failed: {str(e)}' with generic message;
  full error now logged server-side only
- demo_login: replaced f'Demo login error: {e}' with generic 'Demo login error';
  removed accidental duplicate _log import

WHY: SecureByDesign v1.1.0 audit, TIER-2 STANDARD platform.
Ref: https://github.com/Yems221/securebydesign-llmskill/blob/main/SKILL.md

commit.message:
chore(compose): restore postgres bind mounts for main/dev (#118)

* chore(compose): restore local postgres bind mounts for main and dev

* Add migration/rollback notes for postgres bind-mount switch (main & dev) (#129)

* docs: add explicit migration/rollback notes for postgres bind-mount change

* chore(compose): ensure postgres bind-mount dirs exist with correct ownership before startup (#127)

* chore(compose): add ensure-bind-mounts scripts and update Makefile/README

* fix(compose): recursive chown and correct success message in ensure-bind-mounts

* fix(scripts): upgrade-postgres supports bind-mount postgres data dirs (main/dev) (#128)

* fix(scripts): update upgrade-postgres to support bind-mount postgres data dirs

main/dev now use host bind mounts (./data/main/postgres, ./data/dev/postgres)
instead of named Docker volumes. The upgrade scripts previously used
`docker volume inspect` which silently found nothing and exited with
"no existing data to migrate" — even when old-version data existed in
the bind-mount directory, creating a risk of starting a new Postgres
major version against incompatible on-disk data.

Changes:
- Add POSTGRES_DATA_DIR to .env.main and .env.dev pointing at the
  bind-mount path; uat/prod env files unchanged (they use named volumes)
- In both upgrade-postgres.sh and .ps1:
  - Parse POSTGRES_DATA_DIR; set USE_BIND_MOUNT flag
  - Bind-mount path: check directory emptiness instead of docker volume inspect
  - Read PG_VERSION directly from host filesystem (no extra alpine container)
  - Pass absolute bind-mount path to the backup temp container's -v flag
  - Rename (mv/Move-Item) the old data dir to .bak.TIMESTAMP instead of
    docker volume rm; wrap with explicit error messages
  - Update header, confirmation step 3, and done-summary for each mode
- uat/prod named-volume code path is completely unaffected

* fix(compose): use ${POSTGRES_DATA_DIR} in main/dev postgres volume definitions

Replace hardcoded bind-mount paths with ${POSTGRES_DATA_DIR} in
docker-compose.main.yml and docker-compose.dev.yml, making the env
variable the single source of truth for both the running container and
the upgrade-postgres migration scripts. Update env-file comments to
document this relationship.

* Update docker-compose.dev.yml

* docs: update postgres and LEI persistence docs to reflect dev/main bind mounts, fix LEI_DATADIR variable, add bash+PowerShell variants (#126)

* docs: update postgres persistence docs to reflect dev bind mount at ./data/dev/postgres

* Update docs/environments/multi-environment-quickstart.md

* docs: add main env coverage, fix LEI_DATADIR var name, add bash+PS versions for data management

* docs(lei): fix ordered list prefixes and table alignment for markdownlint

---------

'http://fileserver.pingcap.net'`

```groovy
// Removed:
environment {
    FILE_SERVER_URL = 'http://fileserver.pingcap.net'
}
```

No behavioral change — the variable had no callers.

Add Monthly Amortized Cost chart and update AWS Benchmark metrics (#2077)

Fixes OPS-3858.

This PR uses the latest exported version of the baseline analytics
dashboard.

Here's a summary of the existing chart updates:

- Estimated Monthly Savings — Removed the green cell highlight that
appeared when savings exceeded zero. The number now displays cleanly
without background coloring.

- Top Opportunities to Address — Added proper percentage formatting for
the "Percent" column, so values display as e.g. 12.5% instead of a raw
decimal.

- Unified Cost Efficiency Metric — Two improvements:

1. The underlying calculation now handles edge cases (zero cost,
negative or out-of-range results) more gracefully, returning 0 instead
of crashing or showing unexpected values.

2. The color coding was upgraded from a single red threshold to a
three-tier system: red (below 80%), yellow (80–90%), and green (90%+),
giving a clearer at-a-glance health signal.

Fix PC0013 false positive on Option field member access (#127)

* Fix PC0013 false positive on Option field member access (#112)

When a .Get() argument uses an option member access expression like
RecordVar."OptionField"::Value, and the accessed field matches the
primary key field at the same argument position, the diagnostic is
now correctly suppressed.

Added IsOptionMemberAccessOnMatchingPrimaryKeyField helper that
unwraps IConversionExpression to find IOptionAccess, then verifies
the IFieldAccess instance field name matches the primary key field.

Also created the missing C# test class for RecordGetProcedureArguments
wiring all 34 existing + 2 new test cases.

* Harden PC0013 option access check with table type verification

Verify that the IFieldAccess in the option member access expression
belongs to the same table as the .Get() invocation, preventing
false negatives when two different tables share the same field name
in their primary key (cross-table scenario).

Added test: RecordGetOptionMemberAccessCrossTable.al

commit.message:
kernel/patches: fix brcmfmac netdev registration BUG and ip-a hang

Add patch 0002 to fix a kernel BUG in brcmfmac triggered on boot with
the BCM4330 SDIO chip (AP6330 WiFi, Cubieboard4):

  kernel BUG at net/core/dev.c:10174
  BUG_ON(dev->reg_state != NETREG_UNINITIALIZED)

The BUG fires in brcmf_net_attach() → register_netdev() because
brcmf_fweh_activate_events() is called inside brcmf_cfg80211_attach()
before brcmf_net_attach() returns. A concurrent firmware E_IF event can
race through brcmf_notify_ifevent() → brcmf_net_attach(), registering
the ndev. When the original brcmf_net_attach() then also calls
register_netdev() on the same ndev, the kernel BUGs on the already-set
reg_state.

The BUG also permanently deadlocks rtnl_lock, making every subsequent
'ip a' / 'ip link' command hang forever.

Fix: change the locked=false path in brcmf_net_attach() to acquire
rtnl_lock + wiphy_lock and call cfg80211_register_netdevice() instead
of register_netdev(). This ensures wdev->registered is set to true
before register_netdevice() runs, which suppresses the concurrent
NETDEV_REGISTER notifier path and prevents the race.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Pipeline] Fix RunHistoryTests: correct assertions and stub registration (#400)

Closes #399

## Summary

Fixes 2 failing tests in `PRDtoProd.Tests.RunHistoryTests` that caused
CI to fail on `main`.

## Root Cause

**`EvidenceStrip_ShowsZeroWhenNoRuns`** — The assertion
`Assert.Contains("0 pipeline runs", html)` can never pass because the
evidence strip renders value and label in *separate* `(div)` elements:
```html
(div class="evidence-val")0(/div)
(div class="evidence-lbl")pipeline runs(/div)
```
The string `"0 pipeline runs"` is never a contiguous substring of the
HTML.

**`EvidenceStrip_TotalsMatchAggregatedData`** — `CreateFactory` used
`services.SingleOrDefault(...)` to find and remove the
`IShowcaseService` registration. `SingleOrDefault` throws
`InvalidOperationException` if more than one matching descriptor is
present, and silently skips removal if the wrong descriptor is returned.
This could leave the real `ShowcaseService` in place (which reads from
the filesystem and returns actual showcase data), causing `TotalIssues`
to be ≠ 10 and making `Assert.Contains("10+", html)` fail.

## Changes

- **`PRDtoProd.Tests/RunHistoryTests.cs`**
- `CreateFactory`: Replace `SingleOrDefault` + `Remove` with a
`Where(...).ToList()` loop that removes *all* `IShowcaseService`
descriptors, then adds the stub. More robust and avoids throwing on
multiple registrations.
- `EvidenceStrip_ShowsZeroWhenNoRuns`: Replace `Assert.Contains("0
pipeline runs", html)` with two targeted assertions that match the
actual HTML structure:
    - `Assert.Contains("pipeline runs", html)` — label is rendered
- `Assert.Contains("class=\"evidence-val\">0<", html)` — value div
contains `0`

## PRD Fidelity

No PRD involved — this is a CI bug fix for tests introduced by PR #393.

## Test Results

Changes are minimal and surgical — only test assertions and the factory
stub setup are modified. No production code changed. CI will validate.

commit.message:
Fix NTLM ProcessTargetInfo returning wrong buffer slice when skipping entries (#125201)

`ProcessTargetInfo` in managed NTLM returns trailing zeros instead of
actual target info data when the server challenge includes `TargetName`
or `ChannelBindings` AV pairs (which are skipped and replaced). The
early-exit path was correct; the fallback return was off-by-one in the
wrong direction.

## Changes

- **Bug fix** (`NegotiateAuthenticationPal.ManagedNtlm.cs`):
One-character fix — `AsSpan(targetInfoOffset)` → `AsSpan(0,
targetInfoOffset)`. The old code returned the unused trailing portion of
the pre-allocated buffer; the fix returns the written portion.

- **Test infrastructure** (`FakeNtlmServer.cs`): Added
`SendPreExistingTargetName` and `SendPreExistingChannelBindings`
properties (default `false`). When set, the server challenge includes
dummy `TargetName`/`ChannelBindings` AV pairs that the client must skip
and replace, forcing `targetInfoOffset < targetInfoBuffer.Length` and
hitting the previously dead code path.

- **Regression test** (`NegotiateAuthenticationTests.cs`):
`NtlmWithPreExistingTargetInfoEntriesTest` — `[ConditionalTheory]` gated
on `UseManagedNtlm`, exercises all non-trivial flag combinations
`(true,false)`, `(false,true)`, `(true,true)` and verifies full
authentication succeeds. The test is scoped to managed NTLM platforms
(Ubuntu 24/26, OpenSUSE 16) because the bug lives in the managed
implementation; platforms using the system gss-ntlmssp library may not
handle pre-existing AV pairs in the server challenge consistently across
versions.

# Customer Impact

NTLM authentication fails when the server includes `TargetName` or
`ChannelBindings` entries in the challenge's target info. The corrupted
target info causes HMAC verification to fail on the server side,
breaking authentication entirely for those server configurations.

# Regression

Not a regression introduced in the most recent release — this is a
pre-existing latent bug in the managed NTLM implementation. It was
masked because `FakeNtlmServer` never emitted those AV pair types in
tests.

# Testing

Full NTLM exchange tested with each combination of pre-existing
`TargetName`/`ChannelBindings` entries in the challenge. All 122
existing unit tests continue to pass.

# Risk

Low. The fix is a single character change to a slice argument. The
affected code path was previously unreachable in tests; the new tests
confirm correctness. No protocol logic changed.

# Package authoring no longer needed in .NET 9

IMPORTANT: Starting with .NET 9, you no longer need to edit a NuGet
package's csproj to enable building and bump the version.
Keep in mind that we still need package authoring in .NET 8 and older
versions.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> ## Bug Description
> 
> In
`src/libraries/System.Net.Security/src/System/Net/NegotiateAuthenticationPal.ManagedNtlm.cs`,
the `ProcessTargetInfo` method has a bug on line 563:
> 
> ```csharp
> return targetInfoBuffer.AsSpan(targetInfoOffset).ToArray();
> ```
> 
> This returns the **unused trailing portion** of `targetInfoBuffer`
(from `targetInfoOffset` to the end), when it should return the **used
portion** (from 0 to `targetInfoOffset`). The fix is:
> 
> ```csharp
> return targetInfoBuffer.AsSpan(0, targetInfoOffset).ToArray();
> ```
> 
> ### Context of the bug
> 
> The `ProcessTargetInfo` method:
> 1. Allocates `targetInfoBuffer` with size: `targetInfo.Length + 20 + 4
+ spnSize + 8`
> 2. Copies AV pairs from the input `targetInfo` into
`targetInfoBuffer`, **skipping** any existing `TargetName` or
`ChannelBindings` entries
> 3. Appends its own `TargetName`, `ChannelBindings`, `Flags`, and `EOL`
entries
> 4. Uses `targetInfoOffset` as the write cursor tracking how many bytes
were written
> 
> At the end:
> - Line 558: `if (targetInfoOffset == targetInfoBuffer.Length) return
targetInfoBuffer;` — this is the happy path when no entries were skipped
> - Line 563: `return
targetInfoBuffer.AsSpan(targetInfoOffset).ToArray();` — **BUG**: returns
trailing zeros instead of the actual data
> 
> ### Why existing tests don't catch this
> 
> The `FakeNtlmServer.GenerateChallenge()` in
`src/libraries/Common/tests/System/Net/Security/FakeNtlmServer.cs` never
includes `TargetName` or `ChannelBindings` AV pairs in its challenge
message. Therefore, `ProcessTargetInfo` never skips any entries,
`targetInfoOffset` always equals `targetInfoBuffer.Length`, and the
early return on line 558 always fires. The buggy line 563 is never
reached.
> 
> ### Required changes
> 
> 1. **Fix the bug** in `NegotiateAuthenticationPal.ManagedNtlm.cs` line
563:
> Change `targetInfoBuffer.AsSpan(targetInfoOffset).ToArray()` to
`targetInfoBuffer.AsSpan(0, targetInfoOffset).ToArray()`
> 
> 2. **Add test coverage** by modifying `FakeNtlmServer` in
`src/libraries/Common/tests/System/Net/Security/FakeNtlmServer.cs` to
support optionally including pre-existing `TargetName` and/or
`ChannelBindings` AV pairs in the challenge message's target info. Add a
property like `bool SendPreExistingTargetName { get; set; }` and/or
`bool SendPreExistingChannelBindings { get; set; }` that, when set to
true, inserts dummy `TargetName`/`ChannelBindings` AV pairs into the
challenge's target info before the EOL. This will exercise the code path
where `ProcessTargetInfo` skips entries, causing `targetInfoOffset <
targetInfoBuffer.Length` and reaching the previously-buggy line 563.
> 
> 3. **Add new test(s)** in
`src/libraries/System.Net.Security/tests/UnitTests/NegotiateAuthenticationTests.cs`
that:
> - Create a `FakeNtlmServer` with the pre-existing
TargetName/ChannelBindings feature enabled
>    - Perform a full NTLM exchange and verify authentication succeeds
> - This ensures the trimmed target info buffer is correct when entries
are filtered out
> 
> The tests should be `[ConditionalFact]` or `[ConditionalTheory]` gated
on `IsNtlmAvailable`, consistent with the existing test patterns in the
file.


</details>

docs: update landing page copy for v3.0 libvips / container-first architecture (#1625)

The v3.0 transition to `libvips` (dynamic linking) makes the existing
"Just run one binary!" and "Portability!" feature highlights factually
incorrect — the binary now requires `glibc` and `libvips` runtime
dependencies.

- **`website/content/en/_index.html`**
- "Simple! / Just run one binary!" → "Container-Ready! / Deploy anywhere
effortlessly with our lightweight, official Docker image."
- "Portability! / Manael's binary runs anywhere in a GNU/Linux
environment." → "Powerful Processing! / Powered by libvips, Manael
delivers blazing-fast and memory-efficient image conversions."

- **`website/content/ja/_index.html`**
  - "シンプル！" → "コンテナレディ！" with equivalent Japanese copy
  - "可搬性！" → "パワフルな画像処理！" with equivalent Japanese copy

"High Performance!" (EN) and "高性能！" (JA) blocks are unchanged.

feat: tail call optimization (closes #309) (#363)

Implement proper tail calls (PTC) per ES2015 spec:

- Add TailCall opcode to bytecode instruction set
- Detect tail position calls in bytecode generator (compile_return)
- Propagate tail position through conditional and logical expressions
- Reuse current interpreter frame for tail calls (trampoline loop)
- Support direct tail calls: return f()
- Support conditional tail calls: return cond ? f() : g()
- Support mutual recursion: A calls B in tail position, B calls A
- Prevent stack overflow for recursive tail-position patterns
- Remove tail-call-optimization from Test262 skip list

ci: tiered build→test pipeline with sharding and caching (#984)

Restructure both unit-tests.yml and deploy.yml from monolithic single-job
workflows into multi-stage pipelines:

  build → test-data | test-host | test-rendering | test-jest → deploy → e2e

Key changes:
- Build once, upload artefact, test shards download and run --no-build
- 3 parallel .NET test shards (Data ~1114, Host ~677, Rendering+Runtime ~242)
- Jest tests run in parallel with .NET shards
- NuGet + npm + Playwright browser caching via actions/cache
- Deploy only runs after ALL test shards pass (needs: dependency chain)
- Playwright E2E runs after deploy (separate job)
- Build failure stops pipeline before any tests run

Fixes #971
Fixes #961

commit.message:
feat: Date builtin spec compliance (closes #297) (#361)

Implement the ECMAScript §21.4 Date built-in with:
- Date constructor (new Date(), new Date(value), new Date(year, month, ...))
- Date.now(), Date.parse() (ISO 8601 + legacy formats), Date.UTC()
- All local getters: getFullYear, getMonth, getDate, getDay, getHours,
  getMinutes, getSeconds, getMilliseconds, getTimezoneOffset
- All UTC getters: getUTCFullYear, getUTCMonth, getUTCDate, getUTCDay,
  getUTCHours, getUTCMinutes, getUTCSeconds, getUTCMilliseconds
- All local setters: setTime, setMilliseconds, setSeconds, setMinutes,
  setHours, setDate, setMonth, setFullYear
- All UTC setters: setUTCMilliseconds, setUTCSeconds, setUTCMinutes,
  setUTCHours, setUTCDate, setUTCMonth, setUTCFullYear
- String conversions: toString, toDateString, toTimeString, toISOString,
  toUTCString, toGMTString, toJSON, toLocaleDateString, toLocaleString,
  toLocaleTimeString
- valueOf, getTime
- Invalid date (NaN) propagation throughout all methods
- UTC/local timezone conversion using platform APIs
